title: OOF strategy-state prior plus residual learner
summary: F1 pit decisions are state policies, not just tyre-age thresholds: VSE-style work models pit choice from race state, and dynamic-programming/game-theory papers define decisions from lap-by-lap state.
rationale: F1 pit decisions are state policies, not just tyre-age thresholds: VSE-style work models pit choice from race state, and dynamic-programming/game-theory papers define decisions from lap-by-lap state. Build a leakage-safe empirical prior for P(PitNextLap) from coarse state buckets, then let the main GBDT learn residual deviations.
implementation_hint: Create OOF priors using only training folds, grouped by race-time state such as Year bucket, dry/wet flag, compound, stint, race-progress bucket, tyre-life percentile, laps-remaining bucket, prior-stop count, and dry-rule status. Use hierarchical smoothing/backoff so sparse buckets shrink to broader groups. Feed the prior, logit(prior), and model-minus-prior residual features into the existing classifier.
expected_effect: Should improve calibration of the pit window and reduce variance from hand-picked tyre-life heuristics, especially on rare compounds/race phases.
risk: Big leakage trap if priors are computed on full data or with same-race rows. Over-bucketing can also memorize track-specific quirks and hurt held-out races.
